cases.title: Register with available seat keeps Registered count at or below EventCapacity per testing-plan §3
preconditions: Event is RegistrationOpen with EventCapacity N (e.g. N=5)
registeredCount is strictly less than N
Participant has no active registration for the event
Registration window is active (BR-02 satisfied)
steps: Invoke registrationService.register(eventId, participantId, actorContext)
Query count of registrations in Registered state for the event
cases.expected: Registration transitions to Registered; registeredCount increases by one and remains less than or equal to EventCapacity; INV-02 and BR-03 satisfied immediately after commit (testing-plan §3: Register with available seat → Registered)

cases.title: Sequential registrations fill seats up to EventCapacity without overflow
preconditions: Event is RegistrationOpen with configured EventCapacity N (e.g. N=5)
Zero existing registrations in Registered state
Registration window is active
Each participant has no prior active registration for the event
steps: Register N distinct participants sequentially via registrationService.register(eventId, participantId, actorContext)
After each registration, query count of registrations in Registered state for the event in the database
cases.expected: Each of the N registrations receives state Registered; after the Nth registration Registered count equals EventCapacity exactly; no registration assigns Registered when count would exceed capacity

cases.title: When event is at capacity, new accept routes to waitlist without increasing Registered count
preconditions: Event EventCapacity N with exactly N registrations in Registered state
waitlistEnabled true
Registration window active
New participant with no active registration
steps: Register one additional participant via registrationService.register(eventId, participantId, actorContext)
Query count of registrations in Registered state for the event
cases.expected: New registration receives Waitlisted (not Registered); Registered count remains N (equals EventCapacity); BR-03 invariant holds

cases.title: Waitlist promotion on seat release keeps Registered count at or below EventCapacity
preconditions: Event EventCapacity 2 with two Registered participants and one Waitlisted
waitlistEnabled true
One Registered participant can cancel before CancellationDeadline (BR-07)
steps: Record beforeState: Registered count = 2, Waitlisted count = 1
Cancel one Registered registration to release a seat
Allow FIFO waitlist promotion atomically within the cancellation transaction (BR-08b)
Record afterState: Registered count and participant states
cases.expected: afterState Registered count remains 2 (equals EventCapacity); promoted participant moves from Waitlisted to Registered; total Registered never exceeds EventCapacity during or after promotion

cases.title: Concurrent POST registrations and organizer GET list confirm Registered count at capacity ceiling
preconditions: Published event with registration opened, EventCapacity 3, waitlistEnabled true
Ten or more distinct participant JWTs with no prior registration
Each POST uses a unique Idempotency-Key header
Valid OrganizerAdmin JWT scoped to the event
steps: Fire parallel POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations from all participants with Authorization bearer token and Idempotency-Key headers
Count response bodies with state Registered versus Waitlisted
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations?state=Registered&page=1&pageSize=100 with organizer bearer token
cases.expected: Exactly 3 POST responses return HTTP 200 with body containing state Registered; remaining successful POST responses return HTTP 200 with state Waitlisted and waitlistPosition >= 1; organizer GET returns HTTP 200 paginated envelope with items, total, page, pageSize, and totalPages where total Registered count equals EventCapacity (3) and never greater

cases.title: Flow B lifecycle maintains capacity ceiling through cancel and promotion
preconditions: E2E Flow B fixture: full event (EventCapacity 1), one Registered, one Waitlisted
Organizer and participant tokens available
waitlistEnabled true
steps: Execute Flow B: Registered participant POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations/{registrationId}/cancel with Idempotency-Key header
Verify waitlisted participant is promoted to Registered via GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registration-status
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations?state=Registered as organizer at each step
cases.expected: After promotion exactly one Registered participant exists; count equals EventCapacity (1) and never exceeds it at any step in the flow

cases.title: Capacity under heavy concurrent requests never exceeds EventCapacity per testing-plan §3
preconditions: Event is RegistrationOpen with EventCapacity 3 and waitlistEnabled true
Ten or more distinct participants with no prior registration
Registration window is active (BR-02 satisfied)
steps: Fire parallel registrationService.register(eventId, participantId, actorContext) calls from all participants simultaneously
Query count of registrations in Registered state for the event in the database after all commits settle
cases.expected: Registered count is at most 3 and equals 3 when all attempts succeed; remaining registrations are Waitlisted; testing-plan §3 scenario row satisfied: capacity under heavy concurrent requests never exceeds configured capacity

cases.title: Heavy concurrent burst (12+ attempts, capacity 3) never exceeds EventCapacity
preconditions: Event RegistrationOpen with EventCapacity 3 and waitlistEnabled true
12 or more unique participantIds with no prior registration
Parallel registrationService.register(eventId, participantId, actorContext) calls fired simultaneously
steps: Execute Promise.allSettled on all concurrent register calls
Query count of registrations in Registered state for the event in the database
cases.expected: Registered count is at most 3 and equals 3 when all attempts succeed; exactly (attempts - capacity) registrations are Waitlisted; zero overflow beyond EventCapacity under contention

cases.title: Post-insert capacity guard rolls back transaction on CAPACITY_EXCEEDED
preconditions: Race condition or guard bypass attempt where insert would push Registered count above EventCapacity
Registration module uses transactional BEGIN/COMMIT with post-insert count check per validation-rules BR-03
steps: Trigger registration accept/promotion path that detects postCount > EventCapacity after insert
Observe error and database state after rollback
cases.expected: ApiError with code CAPACITY_EXCEEDED and HTTP 422 (semantic rule violation per error-handling taxonomy); transaction rolled back; no persisted Registered row causes count to exceed EventCapacity

cases.title: Promotion attempt when already at capacity is rejected with CAPACITY_EXCEEDED
preconditions: Event at full capacity with no available seats
Waitlisted participant exists but no seat was released
Manual or erroneous promotion path invoked without prior cancellation
steps: Attempt to promote Waitlisted registration to Registered without a seat release
Query Registered count before and after
cases.expected: Promotion rejected with CAPACITY_EXCEEDED; Registered count unchanged and equals EventCapacity; BR-03 invariant preserved

cases.title: EventCapacity boundary of 1 allows exactly one Registered participant
preconditions: Event EventCapacity 1, RegistrationOpen, waitlistEnabled true
Three distinct participants with no prior registration
steps: Register first participant via registrationService.register(eventId, participantId, actorContext) → expect Registered
Register second and third participants concurrently via registrationService.register
Query count of registrations in Registered state after each attempt
cases.expected: Exactly one Registered row exists at all times; additional participants receive Waitlisted; Registered count never exceeds 1

cases.title: Full event without waitlist rejects registration without creating Registered row
preconditions: Event EventCapacity 2 with two Registered participants
waitlistEnabled false
New participant with valid JWT
Fresh Idempotency-Key header value
steps: POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations for the new participant with Authorization bearer token and Idempotency-Key header
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations?state=Registered as organizer
cases.expected: HTTP 422 with error.code REGISTRATION_REJECTED_FULL, user-safe message, requestId, and timestamp per standard error contract; Registered count remains 2 (at capacity, not above); no Registered row created for rejected request

cases.title: Waitlisted and other non-Registered states are excluded from seat-holder count
preconditions: Event at EventCapacity with multiple Waitlisted registrations
Registration window still open
steps: Query count of registrations in Registered state for the event
Separately count rows in Waitlisted, Cancelled, and other non-Registered states
cases.expected: Registered count equals EventCapacity exactly; Waitlisted rows do not inflate Registered count toward or beyond capacity

cases.title: EventCapacity zero permits no Registered participants
preconditions: Event configured with EventCapacity 0 (valid per CapacityLimit totalSeats >= 0)
RegistrationOpen with waitlistEnabled true or false
Participant with no prior registration
steps: Attempt registrationService.register(eventId, participantId, actorContext) for a participant
Query count of registrations in Registered state for the event
cases.expected: No registration receives Registered state; participant is Waitlisted or rejected per waitlist policy; Registered count remains 0 and never exceeds EventCapacity

cases.title: Capacity validation on accept/promotion executes atomically within one transaction
preconditions: Event with EventCapacity 5
Event rule config row lock serializes concurrent writers per event
steps: Complete a successful registration that assigns Registered via registrationService.register(eventId, participantId, actorContext)
Verify BEGIN/COMMIT wraps insert, post-insert Registered count check, audit write, and waitlist enqueue (if any)
Trigger a failed capacity guard and confirm ROLLBACK
cases.expected: No intermediate state visible where Registered count exceeds EventCapacity; failed BR-03 guard results in full ROLLBACK with no partial seat consumption

cases.title: Unauthenticated registration attempts do not inflate Registered count
preconditions: Event RegistrationOpen with EventCapacity 5 and one existing Registered participant
No Authorization header on registration requests
steps: Fire multiple parallel POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations without Authorization header
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations?state=Registered as organizer
cases.expected: All unauthenticated POST requests return HTTP 401 with standard error envelope (code, message, requestId, timestamp); organizer GET shows Registered count unchanged at 1; no unauthorized registration rows affect capacity invariant

cases.title: Organizer capacity meter shows Registered count never above EventCapacity
preconditions: Organizer logged in viewing event operations dashboard for an event at or near capacity (FR-22)
Event has known EventCapacity N and up to N Registered participants
steps: Navigate to organizer event operations dashboard showing capacity meter and registration summary
Observe registeredSeats versus capacity display
Trigger additional participant registration up to capacity via API or second browser session
Refresh dashboard and verify registeredSeats and capacity fields
cases.expected: Dashboard capacity summary shows registeredSeats less than or equal to capacity at all times; when full, capacity meter indicates no remaining seats without showing registeredSeats greater than capacity; values align with GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/dashboard contract

cases.title: Concurrent waitlist promotion attempts never push Registered count above EventCapacity
preconditions: Event EventCapacity 1 with one Registered and two Waitlisted participants
waitlistEnabled true
Registered participant cancels releasing exactly one seat
steps: Fire parallel promotion paths triggered by the single seat release (e.g. concurrent cancel side-effects or retrying promotion handlers)
Query count of registrations in Registered state after all attempts settle
cases.expected: Exactly one promotion succeeds; Registered count equals EventCapacity (1) and never exceeds it; losing concurrent attempts return WAITLIST_ORDER_CONFLICT (HTTP 409) or equivalent retry-safe outcome without creating extra Registered rows

cases.title: Capacity increase triggers waitlist promotion without exceeding new EventCapacity
preconditions: Event RegistrationOpen with EventCapacity 2, two Registered, two Waitlisted
waitlistEnabled true
Organizer Admin increases EventCapacity to 4 with mandatory audit per BR-22
steps: Record beforeState: Registered count = 2, Waitlisted count = 2
Apply capacity increase creating two available seats and triggering FIFO waitlist promotion (FR-12)
Record afterState: Registered and Waitlisted counts
cases.expected: afterState Registered count equals 4 (new EventCapacity) and never exceeds it; exactly two waitlisted participants promoted FIFO; remaining Waitlisted count is 0; BR-03 invariant holds throughout capacity increase and promotion

cases.title: CheckedIn seat-holder cancellation promotes FIFO while maintaining capacity ceiling
preconditions: Event EventCapacity 2 with two CheckedIn participants and one Waitlisted
waitlistEnabled true
One CheckedIn participant can cancel before CancellationDeadline (BR-07)
steps: Record beforeState: Registered/CheckedIn seat-holder count = 2, Waitlisted count = 1
Cancel one CheckedIn registration to release a seat (BR-08 seat-holder path)
Allow FIFO waitlist promotion atomically within the cancellation transaction (BR-08b)
Record afterState: seat-holder count and participant states
cases.expected: afterState seat-holder count remains 2 (equals EventCapacity); promoted participant moves from Waitlisted to Registered; total Registered never exceeds EventCapacity during or after promotion
